what_it_does: An IIFE that seeds the CV_AI registry's built-in data that does NOT depend on the composer's prompt helpers: three providers (claude, openai-image, vision), three behaviours (voice.conceptv, angle, diversity), eight skills (transform skills + a theme skill), four context resolvers (generic/pages/widget/wizard), and two image capabilities (image.generate, image.edit). Capabilities needing composer helpers are seeded elsewhere (AIEngine.jsx). Sets window.CV_AI._seeded at the end.
observations: Throws on load if CV_AI is missing ('load app/ai/ai-registry.js first') — loud load-order enforcement.

Providers: claude (text/stream, json, maxPromptChars 200000), openai-image (via window.cvOpenAI), and vision (multimodal, caps.exportOnly:true — unavailable in browser sandbox, callers fall back to local pixel analysis).

voice.conceptv behaviour carries the house voice as a fixed text fragment (sentence case, second person, no exclamation/emoji, concrete numbers, canonical product names: User Portal, Property Wizard, Virtual Hub, Capture, Vi).
angle behaviour is a single parametric entry: text is a function of params.angle mapping shorter/formal/specific/different to instructions, defaulting to a distinct-angle instruction.
diversity behaviour seeds parallel single-shot generations to differ (uses params.seed and slotIndex).
SKILLS array defines seven transform skills (shorten/lengthen/urgent/friendly/pro/audit/concrete) all targeting capability 'doc.transform'; plus skill.theme targeting 'theme.generate'.

Four context resolvers keyed by surface: context.generic (floor), context.pages (deck/brochure — page neighbourhood + hasCta/hasStats), context.widget (kind/system + kpi/media/chart shape), context.wizard (kind + step sequence).
pageNeighbourhood helper renders the page before/current/after with a ▶ marker, self-contained so context resolution works before AIEngine loads.
image.generate/image.edit capabilities run via the resolved openai-image provider, surfaces ['*'], behaviours [].
A 'CV_HOST:ai-entries' marker comment marks where Vi-authored entries (via ds.propose) are inserted above the _seeded flag.
standouts: The ConceptV voice is single-sourced here as one behaviour fragment — every capability composes it rather than inlining voice strings.
The 'angle' behaviour collapses the field toolbar's shorter/formal/specific/different into ONE parametric entry rather than four — parameterisation over duplication.
Context resolvers project 'what screen Vi is on' into a compact prompt context, with a generic floor so no surface is ever context-less.
vision provider is declared but marked exportOnly/unavailable-in-sandbox, with a documented fallback — honest about runtime availability.
conventions: Pure-data seed: every entry parametric and inheritable; adding a provider/behaviour/skill/context is a registration not code (stated in header).
Splits seeding by dependency: helper-free entries here, helper-dependent capabilities in AIEngine.jsx — mirrors how CV_REGISTRY built-ins carrying render() are seeded where the function lives.
All registrations use { silent: true } and a _seeded marker flag is set at the end.
Single-sourcing of the voice and parameterisation of the angle behaviour follow the project's no-second-home rule.
notes: The helper-free built-in seed of CV_AI — the three providers, the single-sourced ConceptV voice + parametric angle/diversity behaviours, the transform/theme skills, and the four surface context resolvers; capabilities needing prompt helpers live in AIEngine.jsx.
questions: The transform skills all target capability 'doc.transform' and skill.theme targets 'theme.generate' — these capabilities are seeded in AIEngine.jsx; what guarantees that module loads so the skills are not dangling?
The vision provider is exportOnly/unavailable in the browser — which callers depend on it and how is the 'local pixel analysis' fallback wired given the registry's otherwise loud-fail stance?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header says it seeds the helper-free built-in providers/behaviours/skills/contexts (+ image capabilities) and defers helper-dependent capabilities to AIEngine.jsx, which is exactly what it does.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth (voice single-sourced, angle parameterised not duplicated)
Configuration as data (every AI primitive is a registration)
Context projection (surface → compact prompt context, with a floor)
Honest runtime availability (vision exportOnly + documented fallback)
intention_signals: Trying to populate the AI registry with the foundational, dependency-free building blocks (endpoints, the house voice, reusable steers, the screen-to-context projectors, the user-invokable transforms) so every Vi move composes from one catalogue rather than inlined functions and strings.
novelty: Collapsing four field-toolbar steers into one parametric 'angle' behaviour, and seeding surface-keyed context resolvers with a guaranteed generic floor — plus declaring a provider (vision) as available-only-on-export with a named fallback.
contribution: Contributes the built-in AI primitives: the provider set, the single-sourced ConceptV voice and parametric angle/diversity behaviours, the transform/theme skill catalogue, the four surface context resolvers (with the pageNeighbourhood helper), and the two image capabilities.
summary_for_embedding: IIFE seeding CV_AI built-ins — providers (claude/openai-image/vision), behaviours (single-sourced ConceptV voice + parametric angle/diversity), transform/theme skills, four surface context resolvers, and two image capabilities.
intention_for_embedding: To seed the AI registry with the foundational dependency-free primitives (endpoints, the house voice, reusable steers, screen-context projectors, user transforms) so every Vi move composes from one single-sourced catalogue.
